name: Trilogy
evidence: Trilogy is an Austin-based enterprise software company founded by billionaire Joe Liemandt (ESW Capital family of software firms); donor occupation 'Software' matches.

name: Alpha school
evidence: Alpha School is an Austin PreK-8 private school ($40k tuition) using the '2 Hour Learning' AI model, tied to the Liemandt/2HL school-choice ecosystem; donor is a 'Principal'.

name: Lake Travis Fire Fighters PAC
evidence: Firefighters' union political action committee (Lake Travis); name unambiguously a labor/firefighter PAC.

name: Pflugerville Firefighters PAC
evidence: Firefighters' union political action committee (Pflugerville); name unambiguously a labor/firefighter PAC.

name: Matthew Michelsen
evidence: Matt Michelsen runs a family office (MCM Family Partners) and is a serial tech angel/venture investor; donor occupation 'Family Office' matches, though he is San Diego-based.

name: Friends of the Expo Center PAC
evidence: Political committee whose name references the Travis County Exposition Center (home of Rodeo Austin); appears to be an advocacy/campaign committee, but no filing detail confirmed.

name: Minton Bassett Flores & Carsey
evidence: Minton, Bassett, Flores & Carsey, P.C. is an Austin law firm (criminal defense, family law, civil litigation) operating since 1963.

name: International Association of Fire Fighters PAC
evidence: IAFF is the national firefighters' labor union; its PAC is a labor political committee.

name: KCP Inc.
evidence: Generic acronym company; donor self-reported 'Self employed'. Could not confirm the specific firm or its industry.

name: DEC PAC
evidence: Ambiguous PAC acronym (could be a Democratic Executive Committee or a professional-association PAC); could not confirm which organization.

name: Aurea
evidence: Aurea Software is an Austin enterprise-software company (an ESW Capital affiliate, sibling to Trilogy); donor occupation 'CEO'.

name: Bucee's
evidence: Buc-ee's is the Texas travel-center/convenience-store chain; donors are 'Owner/Co-founder' (Arch 'Beaver' Aplin III founded the chain).

name: TelcoDR, Inc.
evidence: TelcoDR is a Texas-based telecom cloud software/investment company founded by Danielle Royston (also CEO of SaaS venture Totogi); donor occupation 'CEO'.

name: AHCV
evidence: AHCV = Adelanto HealthCare Ventures, a healthcare-reimbursement/Medicaid finance consultancy (Austin office) led by CEO Carlos Zaffirini; focus on safety-net/Medicaid programs.

name: Legend Communities
evidence: Legend Communities, Inc. is a Lakeway/Austin master-planned real estate developer (Rough Hollow Lakeway, The Square at Lohmans); principals Haythem Dawlett and John Scardino; donor is 'Developer/Principal'.

name: O.P.E.R.A.T.E.
evidence: Appears to be a political committee (acronym PAC); could not confirm the sponsoring organization or industry.

name: Roger Beasley Investments
evidence: Investment/holding entity tied to the Roger Beasley automotive dealership group (Mazda/Hyundai/Mitsubishi) in Austin; classified by the underlying auto-retail business.

name: Haveli Investments
evidence: Haveli Investments is an Austin private-equity firm (software/gaming buyouts) founded in 2021 by ex-Vista president Brian Sheth; $4.5B debut fund; donor occupations Investor/Finance.

name: KPW PAC
evidence: Unidentified PAC acronym; could not confirm sponsoring organization or industry.

name: LKCM
evidence: LKCM = Luther King Capital Management, a Fort Worth investment-advisory firm (with an Austin office) managing ~$28B; donor occupations Portfolio Manager/Investor.

name: Marrs Ellis & Hodge LLP
evidence: Marrs Ellis & Hodge LLP is an Austin/Houston litigation boutique (eminent domain, fiduciary/property disputes); donors are Partner/Lawyer.

name: Halff Associates State PAC
evidence: Corporate PAC of Halff Associates, a large Texas (Richardson) engineering/architecture/surveying firm founded 1950.

name: Favorite Brands
evidence: Favorite Brands is a family-owned Texas beer/wine/spirits distributor (LaMantia family, serves Austin/San Antonio/Dallas/Houston); donors 'Beer Manager/Owner'. Wholesale beverage distribution.

name: ePsolutions Inc
evidence: ePsolutions, Inc. is an Austin software company automating utility-company back-office/CRM operations (founded 2004); donor occupation 'CEO'.

name: Austin Firefighters Assoc PAC
evidence: Political committee of the Austin Firefighters Association (firefighters' labor union).

name: Lake Flato
evidence: Lake|Flato Architects is a nationally recognized San Antonio architecture firm (founded 1984) with an Austin office; donor is 'Office Manager'.

name: LawBridge
evidence: LawBridge is an Austin legal-services company building a vetted attorney network offering affordable legal services/document generation; donor occupation 'Attorney'.

name: Tames partners
evidence: Donor self-reports 'Real estate developer'; consistent with Austin real-estate figure Kirk Tames, though the specific firm 'Tames Partners' was not independently confirmed.

name: JPM Ranch Company
evidence: JPM Ranch Company is a Montague County, TX cattle/quarter-horse ranch owned by Jack Martin (former Hill+Knowlton CEO) & Patsy Woods Martin; the family platform (JPM Advisory) is also active in oil & gas. Ranching has no taxonomy label.

name: AMBL-IEM,LLC
evidence: Unidentified LLC; donor occupation 'Partner'. Could not confirm the business or its industry.

name: Luther King Company Management Corporation
evidence: Same as LKCM: Luther King Capital Management, Fort Worth investment-advisory firm (offices incl. Austin); donor occupation 'Investment Advisor'.

name: LAN-PAC
evidence: Corporate PAC of Lockwood, Andrews & Newnam, Inc. (LAN), a Texas civil-engineering firm founded 1935.

name: ABML
evidence: Unidentified acronym company; donor occupation 'President/Owner'. Could not confirm the business or industry.

name: iFLY
evidence: iFLY Indoor Skydiving is an Austin-headquartered indoor-skydiving (wind-tunnel) recreation/attraction company; donor occupation 'engineer' fits iFLY Holdings' tunnel engineering.

name: GoodLeap
evidence: GoodLeap (formerly Loanpal) is a sustainability-focused fintech lender financing residential solar/clean-energy home improvements; donor is 'CEO Founder Chairman' (Hayes Barnard).

name: Carpenter & Associates Inc
evidence: Carpenter & Associates, Inc. is an Austin full-service real estate brokerage/development/investment firm (founded 1971); donor occupation 'Real Estate Investor'.

name: Asuragen
evidence: Asuragen is an Austin molecular-diagnostics company (genetic carrier screening, oncology testing kits), now a Bio-Techne brand; donor occupation 'Chairman'.

name: Rudner Law Offices
evidence: Rudner Law Offices (Dallas) is a hospitality-industry law firm representing hotels/resorts/conference centers; founder/owner Steve Rudner; donor occupation 'Owner'.

name: Kleinheinz Capital Partners
evidence: Kleinheinz Capital Partners was a Fort Worth global long/short hedge fund founded by John Kleinheinz (peaked ~$4B AUM); Kleinheinz is a prominent Republican mega-donor. Donor occupation 'CEO-President'.

name: Kirfman, Jack, Sec. - Vote PAC
evidence: Appears to be a union/labor 'Vote' PAC (secretary Jack Kirfman); sample occupation 'Employee Organizing' supports a labor committee, but the specific sponsoring union was not confirmed.
